title: Transformation of China's Iron Ore Arrivals
section_path: Transformation of China's Iron Ore Arrivals
section_path_text: Transformation of China's Iron Ore Arrivals
summary:
Main
Image reference: assets/2025-11-12_transformation-of-chinas-iron-ore-arrivals_img_rgb-color-brs-shipbrokers-monogram-l_c365f35f1de5.png
On 27 October, the 324,972 Dwt bulk carrier SM GEMINI1 on completing a voyage from Brazil, berthed at the second Dongjiakou Port iron ore terminal, Qingdao, thereby marking the commissioning this 400,000-ton ore terminal, which began construction in December 2022. Dongjiakou thus became the second port, after Zhoushan, to have a dual 400,000-ton ore terminal configuration, and the first in northern China. In its never-ending quest to achieve economies of scale, the shipping market has continuously pursued vessel enlargement, giving birth to 400,000 Dwt Valemax bulk carriers, specifically designed to transport long-haul iron ore from Brazil to China.
